source_file: /home/jonaswsl/projects/daygame-coach/data/05.EXT.audio-features/coach_kyle_how_to_approach_a_girl/How to Fix Rejection in 5 minutes (INFIELD) [JKWRj2hwbT4]/How to Fix Rejection in 5 minutes (INFIELD) [JKWRj2hwbT4].audio.asr.clean16k.audio_features.json

video_type.reasoning: Multiple distinct approaches to different targets (bad demos + one good approach) with commentary/breakdowns between each. Coach deliberately demonstrates bad and good delivery across several interactions.

transcript_confidence.reasoning: ASR text is coherent and follows logically, but speaker diarization is poor — SPEAKER_00 captures nearly all speech including target responses. Only 2 segments assigned to SPEAKER_01. This makes speaker attribution unreliable without manual override.

SPEAKER_00.reasoning: mixed_diarization_error: SPEAKER_00 contains both coach speech (openers, commentary, camera address) AND target responses (short answers like 'thank you', 'good', giving name). Pyannote merged coach and target into one speaker ID.

SPEAKER_01.reasoning: Only 2 segments during the good approach. 'Yeah, I really liked the outfit' continues the coach's compliment. 'Good to see you' is a coach-style positive remark. Likely the coach captured under a second speaker ID momentarily.
